tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceAssociateApplicationFleet.associateApplicationFleet
tools.description: Associates the specified application with the specified fleet. This is only supported for Elastic fleets.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceAssociateApplicationToEntitlement.associateApplicationToEntitlement
tools.description: Associates an application to entitle.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceAssociateFleet.associateFleet
tools.description: Associates the specified fleet with the specified stack.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceBatchAssociateUserStack.batchAssociateUserStack
tools.description: Associates the specified users with the specified stacks. Users in a user pool cannot be assigned to stacks with fleets that are joined to an Active Directory domain.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceBatchDisassociateUserStack.batchDisassociateUserStack
tools.description: Disassociates the specified users from the specified stacks.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCopyImage.copyImage
tools.description: Copies the image within the same region or to a new region within the same AWS account. Note that any tags you added to the image will not be copied.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateAppBlock.createAppBlock
tools.description:
Creates an app block.

App blocks are an Amazon AppStream 2.0 resource that stores the details about the virtual hard disk in an S3 bucket. It also stores the setup script with details about how to mount the virtual hard disk. The virtual hard disk includes the application binaries and other files necessary to launch your applications. Multiple applications can be assigned to a single app block.

This is only supported for Elastic fleets.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateApplication.createApplication
tools.description:
Creates an application.

Applications are an Amazon AppStream 2.0 resource that stores the details about how to launch applications on Elastic fleet streaming instances. An application consists of the launch details, icon, and display name. Applications are associated with an app block that contains the application binaries and other files. The applications assigned to an Elastic fleet are the applications users can launch.

This is only supported for Elastic fleets.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateDirectoryConfig.createDirectoryConfig
tools.description: Creates a Directory Config object in AppStream 2.0. This object includes the configuration information required to join fleets and image builders to Microsoft Active Directory domains.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateEntitlement.createEntitlement
tools.description: Creates a new entitlement. Entitlements control access to specific applications within a stack, based on user attributes. Entitlements apply to SAML 2.0 federated user identities. Amazon AppStream 2.0 user pool and streaming URL users are entitled to all applications in a stack. Entitlements don't apply to the desktop stream view application, or to applications managed by a dynamic app provider using the Dynamic Application Framework.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateFleet.createFleet
tools.description: Creates a fleet. A fleet consists of streaming instances that your users access for their applications and desktops.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateImageBuilder.createImageBuilder
tools.description:
Creates an image builder. An image builder is a virtual machine that is used to create an image.

The initial state of the builder is PENDING. When it is ready, the state is RUNNING.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateImageBuilderStreamingUrl.createImageBuilderStreamingUrl
tools.description: Creates a URL to start an image builder streaming session.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateStack.createStack
tools.description: Creates a stack to start streaming applications to users. A stack consists of an associated fleet, user access policies, and storage configurations.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateStreamingUrl.createStreamingUrl
tools.description: Creates a temporary URL to start an AppStream 2.0 streaming session for the specified user. A streaming URL enables application streaming to be tested without user setup.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateUpdatedImage.createUpdatedImage
tools.description:
Creates a new image with the latest Windows operating system updates, driver updates, and AppStream 2.0 agent software.

For more information, see the "Update an Image by Using Managed AppStream 2.0 Image Updates" section in Administer Your AppStream 2.0 Images, in the Amazon AppStream 2.0 Administration Guide.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateUsageReportSubscription.createUsageReportSubscription
tools.description: Creates a usage report subscription. Usage reports are generated daily.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceCreateUser.createUser
tools.description: Creates a new user in the user pool.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteAppBlock.deleteAppBlock
tools.description: Deletes an app block.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteApplication.deleteApplication
tools.description: Deletes an application.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteDirectoryConfig.deleteDirectoryConfig
tools.description: Deletes the specified Directory Config object from AppStream 2.0. This object includes the information required to join streaming instances to an Active Directory domain.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteEntitlement.deleteEntitlement
tools.description: Deletes the specified entitlement.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteFleet.deleteFleet
tools.description: Deletes the specified fleet.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteImage.deleteImage
tools.description: Deletes the specified image. You cannot delete an image when it is in use. After you delete an image, you cannot provision new capacity using the image.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteImageBuilder.deleteImageBuilder
tools.description: Deletes the specified image builder and releases the capacity.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteImagePermissions.deleteImagePermissions
tools.description: Deletes permissions for the specified private image. After you delete permissions for an image, AWS accounts to which you previously granted these permissions can no longer use the image.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteStack.deleteStack
tools.description: Deletes the specified stack. After the stack is deleted, the application streaming environment provided by the stack is no longer available to users. Also, any reservations made for application streaming sessions for the stack are released.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteUsageReportSubscription.deleteUsageReportSubscription
tools.description: Disables usage report generation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDeleteUser.deleteUser
tools.description: Deletes a user from the user pool.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeAppBlocks.describeAppBlocks
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more app blocks.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeApplicationFleetAssociations.describeApplicationFleetAssociations
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more application fleet associations. Either ApplicationArn or FleetName must be specified.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeApplications.describeApplications
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more applications.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeDirectoryConfigs.describeDirectoryConfigs
tools.description:
Retrieves a list that describes one or more specified Directory Config objects for AppStream 2.0, if the names for these objects are provided. Otherwise, all Directory Config objects in the account are described. These objects include the configuration information required to join fleets and image builders to Microsoft Active Directory domains.

Although the response syntax in this topic includes the account password, this password is not returned in the actual response.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeEntitlements.describeEntitlements
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one of more entitlements.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeFleets.describeFleets
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more specified fleets, if the fleet names are provided. Otherwise, all fleets in the account are described.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeImageBuilders.describeImageBuilders
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more specified image builders, if the image builder names are provided. Otherwise, all image builders in the account are described.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeImagePermissions.describeImagePermissions
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes the permissions for shared AWS account IDs on a private image that you own.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeImages.describeImages
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more specified images, if the image names or image ARNs are provided. Otherwise, all images in the account are described.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeSessions.describeSessions
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes the streaming sessions for a specified stack and fleet. If a UserId is provided for the stack and fleet, only streaming sessions for that user are described. If an authentication type is not provided, the default is to authenticate users using a streaming URL.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeStacks.describeStacks
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more specified stacks, if the stack names are provided. Otherwise, all stacks in the account are described.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeUsageReportSubscriptions.describeUsageReportSubscriptions
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more usage report subscriptions.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeUsers.describeUsers
tools.description: Retrieves a list that describes one or more specified users in the user pool.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDescribeUserStackAssociations.describeUserStackAssociations
tools.description:
Retrieves a list that describes the UserStackAssociation objects. You must specify either or both of the following:

  • The stack name

  • The user name (email address of the user associated with the stack) and the authentication type for the user

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDisableUser.disableUser
tools.description: Disables the specified user in the user pool. Users can't sign in to AppStream 2.0 until they are re-enabled. This action does not delete the user.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDisassociateApplicationFleet.disassociateApplicationFleet
tools.description: Disassociates the specified application from the fleet.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDisassociateApplicationFromEntitlement.disassociateApplicationFromEntitlement
tools.description: Deletes the specified application from the specified entitlement.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceDisassociateFleet.disassociateFleet
tools.description: Disassociates the specified fleet from the specified stack.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceEnableUser.enableUser
tools.description: Enables a user in the user pool. After being enabled, users can sign in to AppStream 2.0 and open applications from the stacks to which they are assigned.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceExpireSession.expireSession
tools.description: Immediately stops the specified streaming session.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceListAssociatedFleets.listAssociatedFleets
tools.description: Retrieves the name of the fleet that is associated with the specified stack.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceListAssociatedStacks.listAssociatedStacks
tools.description: Retrieves the name of the stack with which the specified fleet is associated.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceListEntitledApplications.listEntitledApplications
tools.description: Retrieves a list of entitled applications.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceListTagsForResource.listTagsForResource
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of all tags for the specified AppStream 2.0 resource. You can tag AppStream 2.0 image builders, images, fleets, and stacks.

For more information about tags, see Tagging Your Resources in the Amazon AppStream 2.0 Administration Guide.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceStartFleet.startFleet
tools.description: Starts the specified fleet.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceStartImageBuilder.startImageBuilder
tools.description: Starts the specified image builder.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceStopFleet.stopFleet
tools.description: Stops the specified fleet.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceStopImageBuilder.stopImageBuilder
tools.description: Stops the specified image builder.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceTagResource.tagResource
tools.description:
Adds or overwrites one or more tags for the specified AppStream 2.0 resource. You can tag AppStream 2.0 image builders, images, fleets, and stacks.

Each tag consists of a key and an optional value. If a resource already has a tag with the same key, this operation updates its value.

To list the current tags for your resources, use ListTagsForResource. To disassociate tags from your resources, use UntagResource.

For more information about tags, see Tagging Your Resources in the Amazon AppStream 2.0 Administration Guide.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceUntagResource.untagResource
tools.description:
Disassociates one or more specified tags from the specified AppStream 2.0 resource.

To list the current tags for your resources, use ListTagsForResource.

For more information about tags, see Tagging Your Resources in the Amazon AppStream 2.0 Administration Guide.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceUpdateApplication.updateApplication
tools.description: Updates the specified application.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceUpdateDirectoryConfig.updateDirectoryConfig
tools.description: Updates the specified Directory Config object in AppStream 2.0. This object includes the configuration information required to join fleets and image builders to Microsoft Active Directory domains.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceUpdateEntitlement.updateEntitlement
tools.description: Updates the specified entitlement.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceUpdateFleet.updateFleet
tools.description:
Updates the specified fleet.

If the fleet is in the STOPPED state, you can update any attribute except the fleet name.

If the fleet is in the RUNNING state, you can update the following based on the fleet type:

  • Always-On and On-Demand fleet types

    You can update the DisplayName, ComputeCapacity, ImageARN, ImageName, IdleDisconnectTimeoutInSeconds, and DisconnectTimeoutInSeconds attributes.

  • Elastic fleet type

    You can update the DisplayName, IdleDisconnectTimeoutInSeconds, DisconnectTimeoutInSeconds, MaxConcurrentSessions, SessionScriptS3Location and UsbDeviceFilterStrings attributes.

If the fleet is in the STARTING or STOPPED state, you can't update it.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceUpdateImagePermissions.updateImagePermissions
tools.description: Adds or updates permissions for the specified private image.

tools.name: xAmzTargetPhotonAdminProxyServiceUpdateStack.updateStack
tools.description: Updates the specified fields for the specified stack.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
